job_description:
You will work with data, algorithms, and engineering teams to build scalable and reliable AI-powered solutions.
Responsibilities:
Design, train, and optimise machine learning and AI models.
Build pipelines for model training, validation, and deployment.
Collaborate with data engineers and product teams to integrate AI into applications.
Monitor and improve model performance in production environments.
Research and implement new AI techniques and frameworks.
Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code for AI systems.
Requirements:
Strong programming skills (Python, R, or similar).
Experience with ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn.
Knowledge of data preprocessing, feature engineering, and model evaluation.
Familiarity with deployment pipelines, cloud platforms, and MLOps practices.
Strong problem-solving and communication skills.

Role: Software Engineer (C++) - Remote
Location: 100% Remote (Global)
Compensation: USD 50-95 per hour
Role Overview
One of our clients is partnering with a leading AI research lab to engage experienced management consulting professionals on a project basis. In this role, you will translate real-world consulting workflows into structured tasks, datasets, and evaluation criteria to help improve advanced AI systems’ reasoning and problem-solving capabilities.
This opportunity is ideal for professionals who can work independently, think in structured frameworks, and collaborate asynchronously in a deliverables-driven environment.
Key Responsibilities
Break down end-to-end consulting workflows (problem structuring → analysis → synthesis) into clear task specifications and evaluation rubrics.
Review, edit, and benchmark AI-generated outputs such as market analyses, strategic recommendations, operating models, and executive summaries.
Create high-quality reference examples (e.g., issue trees, analytical models, slide outlines) that reflect professional consulting standards.
Identify logical gaps, unsupported assumptions, and weak reasoning in AI outputs; document failure patterns and improvement guidance.
Collaborate asynchronously with researchers and fellow contributors using structured documentation and written feedback.
Ideal Qualifications
2+ years of experience at a top-tier management consulting firm or reputable boutique consultancy.
Strong understanding of hypothesis-driven problem solving and executive-level synthesis.
Advanced skills in PowerPoint and Excel.
Experience with market sizing, financial/operating analysis, and qualitative research.
Excellent written communication and structured thinking skills.
Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines in a remote setting.
Role Details
Expected commitment: 10+ hours per week.
Deliverables-driven structure with defined quality standards.
Fully remote and flexible scheduling.
Ongoing support available for best practices and clarifications.
Application Process
Submit a brief resume or LinkedIn profile outlining your consulting background and areas of expertise.
Complete a short skills questionnaire.
A time-boxed exercise may be used to assess fit.
Selected candidates will be contacted with next steps and additional project details.
All qualified applicants will be considered without regard to legally protected characteristics. Reasonable accommodations are available upon request.
Contract and Payment Terms
Engagement as an independent contractor.
Fully remote work on a flexible schedule.
Project scope may be extended, shortened, or concluded based on needs and performance.
Work will not involve access to confidential or proprietary information from any employer, client, or institution.
Weekly payments via supported payment platforms based on services rendered.
APPLY NOW!
